faults.name: Gate is not driven high enough
faults.why: A logic-level MOSFET still needs enough Vgs to enhance and pull the load low.
faults.verification_test: Measure Vgs when the control signal is high.
faults.fix_recipe: Use a logic-level MOSFET or raise the gate drive within ratings.

faults.name: Source not tied to ground
faults.why: If the source floats, Vgs is undefined and the MOSFET may never turn on.
faults.verification_test: Measure source voltage with respect to circuit ground.
faults.fix_recipe: Reconnect the source to the common ground node.

faults.name: Inductive load missing flyback diode
faults.why: An inductive load can create a voltage spike that resets or damages the circuit.
faults.verification_test: Observe drain voltage when switching the load off.
faults.fix_recipe: Add a flyback diode or snubber across the inductive load.
